zh.lifeSummary: 當John Hyde II於1439年出生在英國波克夏丹華斯時，他的父親John Hyde20歲，母親Alice Agnes Lydiard24歲。他於1454年與Lady Margaret Booth在英國英格蘭達勒姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1487年9月18日享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Hyde II于1439年出生在英国波克夏丹華斯时，他的父亲John Hyde20岁，母亲Alice Agnes Lydiard24岁。他于1454年与Lady Margaret Booth在英国英格兰達勒姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1487年9月18日享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of various places so called in Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Hampshire, and Middlesex. They were named with Old English hīd ‘hide (of land)’, a variable measure of land, differing from place to place and time to time, and seems to have been originally fixed as the amount necessary to support one (extended) family (Old English hīgan, hīwan ‘household’). The surname may also be topographic for someone living on (and farming) a hide of land. The Hyde family has been in Leinster, Ireland, since the early 13th century and one family has been established in the county of Cork since the 16th century. The name was Gaelicized as both Dalaithíd and de hÍde. Compare Hyder .

variants.name: Dollarhide
meanings: Irish: possibly an altered form of de la Hyde (see Hyde ), which was a prominent surname in Leinster from medieval times onward. Compare Dollahite .

History: Francis Dollahide, born c. 1650–60 in County Dublin, Ireland, was the ancestor of Americans with the surname Dollahide, Dollahite, and Dollarhide. In 1680 he was transported to MD for some youthful misdemeanor. He appears to have been an educated man and rose to be an important landowner and politician, serving in the Lower House of the MD legislature as an elected representative for Baltimore County from c. 1705 to 1721.

variants.name: Hyder
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): status name or topographic name for one who lived on or possessed a hide of land, from Middle English hide + the locative suffix -er. This is a characteristically southeastern English name, synonymous with Middle English atte hide elsewhere (see Hyde ).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Hyde II was born in 1439, in Denchworth, Berkshire, England, his father, John Hyde, was 20 and his mother, Alice Agnes Lydiard, was 24. He married Lady Margaret Booth in 1454, in Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 18 September 1487, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Hyde II est né en 1439, à Denchworth, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, John Hyde, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice Agnes Lydiard, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Booth en 1454, à Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 18 septembre 1487, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Hyde II 1439 in Denchworth, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Hyde 20 und seine Mutter Alice Agnes Lydiard 24. Er hat 1454 in Durham, England, United Kingdom Lady Margaret Booth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 18. September 1487 im Alter von 48 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Hyde II è nato il nel 1439 nel Denchworth, Berkshire, England, il suo padre, John Hyde, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Alice Agnes Lydiard, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margaret Booth nel 1454, nel Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 18 settembre 1487, all'età di 48 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Hyde II(이)가 1439년 영국 버크실르 덴ch워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hyde(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Agnes Lydiard(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람에서 Lady Margaret Booth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1487년 9월 18일 48세의 나이로 사망했다.
